_comment: Connector-specific edits to GENERATED files that a cli-printing-press reprint must NOT clobber. check_handfixes.py asserts every marker below is still present (and banned anti-patterns absent) and fails CI if a reprint dropped one. status: active = connector-only, a reprint would clobber it; upstreamed = also fixed in the press, a reprint from a fixed press regenerates it, but the back-port must still be present today.

handfixes.summary: Live x360Recover emits Python-style `id_` keys; primary-key resolution must include id_
handfixes.why: The live API keys records `id_` (not `id`) for devices/appliances/vaults/users. Without id_ in the fallback list, extraction falls through to `name` (wrong primary key) or returns "" (0 rows / all_items_failed_id_extraction). Verified live; the 4.24 templates do not emit id_.
handfixes.spec_encode_followup: Best durable home is a spec annotation (`x-resource-id: id_` on the affected resources) so the press emits the fallback by construction. The generated code itself recommends this ('Annotate the spec with x-resource-id to fix').

handfixes.summary: autoverify items have no native id (synthesized); org-level device rows may omit client_id (injected)
handfixes.why: `/device/{device_id}/autoverify` items carry no native identifier, so sync synthesizes one; org-level device rows on the live API may omit client_id, so the typed client_device table injects it. Both are live-discovered quirks marked `Hand-wired:` and absent from press templates.

handfixes.summary: device `--select` examples use the real serialized JSON field paths (id_, current_health_status.status)
handfixes.why: The press emits placeholder `--select id,name,status`; axcient's real serialized keys differ (`id_`, `current_health_status.status`). Corrected in the docs during onboarding (PR #70). A reprint reverts the examples to the placeholder fields.

handfixes.summary: D1: MCP path/query params render through formatMCPParamValue (no scientific-notation 404)
handfixes.why: JSON-number device_id/appliance_id decode to float64; fmt's %v renders 7-digit IDs as 1.234567e+06, so the URL 404s. Fixed upstream in press 4.24 (formatMCPParamValue); this is the surgical back-port until a reprint absorbs it.

handfixes.summary: D3: sync --dry-run does not mutate sync_state (dependent loop + cursor clears guarded)
handfixes.why: The dependent-resource sync loop wrote sync_state unconditionally and --full/--latest-only cleared cursors unconditionally, so a documented side-effect-free dry run corrupted the local bookkeeping. Fixed in the press via mvanhorn/cli-printing-press#2946; surgical back-port here.

handfixes.summary: restore_point items key on a per-device timestamp string (restore_point_id); synthesize a collision-safe composite id
handfixes.why: The live /device/{device_id}/restore_point endpoint returns items keyed by restore_point_id (a YYYY_MM_DD_HH_MM_SS timestamp string), not a numeric id. ExtractResourceID's override map and generic fallback list both miss it, so UpsertBatch skipped every item and the restore_point table stored zero rows (all_items_failed_id_extraction); the live `device restore-point` write-through warned 'not cached locally'. restore_point_id is unique within a device but not across the fleet, so the fix composes rp:<device_id>:<restore_point_id> (device_id/parent_id are injected by the sync loop) and falls back to the bare restore_point_id for the live single-device path. Verified live; press 4.24 templates emit neither.
handfixes.spec_encode_followup: No single x-resource-id can express a composite (device_id + restore_point_id) key, so this stays a hand-fix; the closest spec hook would be a per-resource composite-key annotation the press does not yet support. Errors are tracked in the v0.2.1 GitHub issue.

handfixes.summary: flat sync enqueue drops dependent-resource names (autoverify/restore_point/client_device) so `--resources ...,autoverify` does not fail as a flat resource
handfixes.why: Dependent resources have no flat list endpoint (syncResourcePath returns 'unknown sync resource'); they sync per-parent via syncDependentResources. A user may still name a dependent explicitly (e.g. `--resources clients,device,autoverify`, which older docs and other tooling may suggest), so without flatSyncResources a named dependent ran once correctly (dependent pass) and once failing (flat pass) - 'two resources, one fails' - and under --strict the spurious flat failure aborted the run. Systemic press behavior, not axcient-specific; a printing-press-retro tracks the upstream fix. (The canonical refresh command is now bare `axcient-cli sync`; see canonical-sync-refresh-hint.)
handfixes.spec_encode_followup: Fix belongs in the press: the flat-sync enqueue should never include a name it also declares as a dependent/walker resource. Filed upstream as mvanhorn/cli-printing-press#2953; until a reprint absorbs it, flatSyncResources guards it here.

handfixes.summary: --strict / all-failed sync errors name the failing resource(s), not just a count
handfixes.why: The sync exit-policy errors emitted only 'N resource(s) failed to sync' with no resource name, so a --strict failure was undiagnosable (the retester could not tell which resource failed). describeFailedResources appends the captured names. Systemic press behavior; a printing-press-retro tracks the upstream fix.
handfixes.spec_encode_followup: Fix belongs in the press sync template (name failed resources in the exit-policy errors). Filed upstream as mvanhorn/cli-printing-press#2953; until a reprint absorbs it, describeFailedResources guards it here.

handfixes.summary: One canonical local-store refresh command everywhere: bare `axcient-cli sync` (no divergent per-command --resources scopes)
handfixes.why: Issue #86 flagged that the 'refresh the local mirror' step was documented with five different --resources scopes across the quick-start, the morning-sweep recipe, and the per-command help text (clients,device / clients,device,autoverify / clients,device,appliance / clients,device,vault,appliance / bare), so 'refresh the mirror' was ambiguous depending on which surface the reader followed. Bare `axcient-cli sync` pulls every top-level resource plus all dependents (parentFilter empty => syncDependentResources runs them all), so it is the complete, canonical refresh. The press generic hint already used bare `axcient-cli sync`; only the hand-authored novel commands and docs diverged. A reprint regenerating the novels (or re-authoring the docs) must not reintroduce a scoped --resources hint.

handfixes.summary: `search` defaults to a concise id/name/type/match projection (not whole raw records); --full restores raw. Logic lives in hand-authored files so a reprint can't clobber it.
handfixes.why: Issue #84/#101 residual item: `search` dumped entire raw JSON records, a token sink for agents. The fix adds store.SearchHits (returns resource_type alongside data) and a renderSearchResults projection, both in HAND-AUTHORED files (internal/store/search_hits.go, internal/cli/search_projection.go) that a cli-printing-press reprint of store.go/search.go won't touch. The generated search.go carries only a minimal hook (--full flag + SearchHits calls + renderSearchResults). A reprint must re-apply that hook; the asserts below fail CI if it regressed. Default-dumping-raw is systemic press behavior (a printing-press retro tracks the upstream fix).
handfixes.spec_encode_followup: Belongs in the press: the generated search command should offer a concise projection by default with a --full opt-in. Until a reprint absorbs it, the hand-authored files + this hook guard it.

handfixes.summary: MCP tool auth-error hints point to the MCP server's env config, not 'Run axcient-cli doctor' (which an MCP-only user can't run)
handfixes.why: Issue #84/#101 residual item: the MCP tool error handlers (internal/mcp/tools.go) told MCP-only users to 'Run axcient-cli doctor' - a CLI command they have no shell access to. doctor stays CLI-only (intentionally excluded from the MCP surface via frameworkCommands); the MCP hints now tell the user to set AXCIENT_API_KEY in the MCP server's configuration. The CLI's own classifyAPIError (internal/cli/helpers.go) KEEPS the doctor hint - correct for CLI users - so this assertion is scoped to tools.go only.

asserts.not_contains: Run 'axcient-cli doctor' to check auth status

asserts.contains: set AXCIENT_API_KEY in this server's configuration

handfixes.summary: resourceStorageID must NOT re-append the parent for the synthesized rp:/av: composite ids (would corrupt the key with a NUL byte); and the hand-authored regression tests guarding the restore_point/autoverify/sync fixes must exist.
handfixes.why: Press 4.24.0 (the #96 fleet re-vendor) added a generic resourceStorageID that appends `id\x00<parent>` for any resource in resourceParentKeyColumns. restore_point and autoverify already synthesize parent-embedding ids (rp:<device_id>:..., av:<device_id>:...), so the generic append double-keyed them into e.g. `rp:dev:rpid\x00dev` - a NUL-containing key that breaks clean-key offline lookups (regressing the #84 Error-1 fix). The fix skips the append ONLY when the id already carries the synthesized rp:/av: prefix; a bare native id still gets the parent composite (so same-id-different-parent rows don't collapse - see TestUpsertBatch_Keys*ByChildAndParent). SEPARATELY, the #96 re-vendor SILENTLY DROPPED the hand-authored regression tests (they are not generated, so the mint replaced the package without them); the fixes' strings survived (asserted elsewhere) but their behavioral guards vanished, which is how the \x00 regression shipped unnoticed. These asserts fail CI if a future reprint re-introduces the double-composite OR drops the tests again. Systemic press behavior - printing-press retro filed (resourceStorageID should skip an already-parent-embedded id; the mint should preserve hand-authored *_test.go).
handfixes.spec_encode_followup: Belongs in the press: resourceStorageID should not append the parent when the resolved id already embeds it, and the mint should not drop hand-authored test files on re-vendor. Until absorbed, this switch + these test-existence asserts guard it.

handfixes.summary: Live restore_point endpoint groups points by Cloud Vault; sync must descend into the nested restore_point[] array
handfixes.why: The per-device /device/{device_id}/restore_point endpoint returns a top-level array of {vault_id, restore_point:[...]} wrappers; the timestamp-string restore_point_id lives only on the inner elements. extractPageItems surfaces the OUTER wrappers (no restore_point_id), so every one fails id extraction (all_items_failed_id_extraction -> 0 rows) - the live #84 symptom that the earlier flat-array regression fixture (0.2.1/0.2.2) never reproduced because it fed the inner shape directly. syncDependentResource now flattens the nested array (flattenRestorePointGroups) before keying, carrying vault_id down onto each point. Live-discovered nested shape, absent from press templates.
handfixes.spec_encode_followup: Belongs in the press: model a parent-grouped sub-collection so the generator descends one level into a named child array by construction. Printing-press retro filed alongside the #84 fix.
